title: viscum album subsp. malyi
intro: Viscum album subsp. malyi, also known as mistletoe, is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of various trees, such as oak and pine. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities. The health benefits of Viscum album subsp. malyi are believed to include improved cardiovascular health, reduced stress and anxiety, and enhanced immune function. Viscum album subsp. malyi is traditionally prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and has also been used in folk medicine as a poultice or tincture.
